⚔️ MISTROVSKÉ DÍLO, KTERÉ NEMÁ KONKURENCI ⚔️

CZ - Zaklínač 3: Divoký hon je pro mě absolutní srdcovka a dost možná nejlepší hra, jakou jsem kdy hrál. Svět, který CD Projekt RED vytvořil, vás kompletně pohltí. Příběh Geralta z Rivie je napsaný tak geniálně, že i obyčejné vedlejší úkoly mají lepší děj než celé jiné hry. Postavy jako Yennefer, Ciri nebo Triss vám přirostou k srdci a na tenhle zážitek jen tak nezapomenete.

A co se týče DLC (Srdce z kamene / O víně a krvi)? To nejsou jen nějaké levné přídavky. Jsou to v podstatě celé další hry, které kvalitou a atmosférou (zejména pohádkový Toussaint) posouvají laťku ještě výš.

Tuhle hru by si měl zahrát povinně každý, kdo to s hraním myslí vážně. Chytlo mě to natolik, že jsem v tom nechal kus života a pokořil všech 78 achievementů na 100 %. 10/10 a dal bych i víc, kdyby to šlo.

⚔️ A MASTERPIECE THAT HAS NO EQUAL ⚔️

ENG - The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt is an absolute masterpiece and quite possibly the best game I have ever played. The world that CD Projekt RED created completely consumes you. Geralt of Rivia's story is so brilliantly written that even simple side quests have better plots than entire other games. Characters like Yennefer, Ciri, or Triss will truly grow on you, and it's an experience you won't easily forget.

And as for the DLCs (Hearts of Stone / Blood and Wine)? These aren't just cheap add-ons. They are basically entire new games that push the bar even higher with their quality and atmosphere (especially the fairytale-like Toussaint).

This game is a must-play for anyone who takes gaming seriously. I loved it so much that I dedicated a huge chunk of my time to it and unlocked all 78 achievements for a 100% completion. 10/10, and I’d give it more if I could.

Ca y'est, je viens enfin de terminer l'aventure principale de the Witcher 3 après environ 120 heures passées dans le jeu, et quelle aventure ! Je l'ai débuté avec en tête de découvrir un classique du médium pour un prix dérisoire en soldes, et j'en ressort un peu amoureuse (de son monde, de ses personnages, de sa narration). Ce jeu ne démérite pas à mes yeux de sa réputation, et je suis sous le charme.


[h3] Quelques défauts de maniabilité [/h3]

[i]Mais commençons cette critique par les défauts qu'on peut lui imputer. Car the Witcher 3 n'en est pas dépourvu, et je peux en un sens comprendre ceux qui n'adhérent pas à la proposition. Simplement, ces défauts n'ont pas affecté mon expérience globale, et passent au second plan de mon appréciation. [/i]

Oui, les contrôles sont parfois un peu rigides - surtout à cheval - et ce serait mentir que de dire que je n'ai jamais été frustrée par la maniabilité ou les passages un peu 'parkour'. J'ai parfois eu envie de jeter ma souris ou ma manette contre l'écran après m'être retrouvée coincée par un mur invisible dans une zone de relief, incapable de rejoindre l'endroit indiqué sur la carte qui se trouve clairement à portée de vue, ou essayé en vain de grimper sur ce putain de bateau, ou m'être retrouvé coincée contre un mur ou un pont à cheval au milieu d'une course ou d'un moment d'histoire important. (Vraiment, la conduite à cheval, autant j'ai vécu de sublimes moments d'exploration très exaltant, autant j'ai parfois eu envie d'envoyer Roach au bûcher). Clairement tout n'est pas parfait de ce point de vue là, et je peux comprendre que certains trouvent ça rédhibitoire. 

[i]Mais j'ai vécu tout ces problèmes comme un léger désagrément parsemant une aventure au combien immersive, à la narration soignée, dans un monde magnifique, au côté d'un personnage incroyable. Je me suis laissée emportée par l'historie et l'univers du jeu, et ce que j'y ai vu / vécu m'a beaucoup parlé [/i].


[h3]Une aventure immersive et inoubliable [/h3]

Déjà, je trouve le monde de Witcher III sublime. Je ne parlerai pas de qualités graphiques à proprement parler - je joue sur un ordinateur portable en qualité basse pour que le jeu tourne au mieux - mais j'ai très souvent eu le souffle coupé par la beauté des paysages. Je ne compte plus le nombre de fois où je me suis juste arrêté pour admirer la vue. Même Velen, qui n'est pas des plus accueillantes avec ses marécages et ses terres désolées par la guerre, peut révéler des charmes cachés dans les lumières du couchant. (La météo de ce jeu, comme son cycle jour / nuit, donnent parfois des panoramas inoubliables). De manière générale, je trouve que le jeu excelle dans l'art d'établir une ambiance pour un lieu. Les marécages hostiles de Velen ne dégagent pas du tout la même atmosphère que l'intrigue mafieuse et politique de Novigrad (surtout la nuit), ou que les champs de tournesols qu'on trouve au Nord de la ville. Et que dire de Skellige ? J'ai passé mes premières heures sur l'île à m'extasier devant chaque falaise, et la majesté de ses montagnes enneigées. Si certains déplacements, surtout en altitude, sont parfois un peu frustrant à Skellige, qu'est-ce que ces îles sont belles, et comme le contraste entre chaque zones est rafraîchissant. 

Si je suis amoureuse de la beauté et l'aspect très immersif / authentique de l'open world, l'écriture mérite elle aussi d'être encensée. Déjà par le soin qui lui a clairement été donné. Chaque cinématique, même pour une quête annexe, propose une mise en scène qui va plus loin que le simple champ/contre-champ, avec parfois des plans très 'cinématique', justement. Les quêtes secondaires et contrats sont parfois drôles, parfois plus sombres, mais questionnent souvent le rôle du Witcher dans ce monde, souvent assez moche et cruel. J'aime beaucoup tout l'aspect moral grise du personnage et des choix qu'il est possible de faire. Au travers de ces quêtes, j'ai fait de ma version de Geralt un sorceleur un peu désabusé, qui penche parfois du côté des monstres opprimés, lui même conscient de ce qu'est d'être un "freak" dans un monde qui pratique la chasse aux sorcières. 

Geralt de Riv fait d'ailleurs aujourd'hui parti de mes protagonistes de jeu vidéo favoris. Comment pourrait-il en être autrement ? Le doublage (anglais dans mon cas) est fantastique, il est terriblement cool lorsqu'il est temps de dézinguer du monstre, mais plus sensible qu'il en à l'air derrière sa façade bourrue. (Sa relation paternelle avec Ciri est subtile mais touchante, et j'ai adoré suivre sa romance avec Yennefer, ou son amitié avec Dandelion). Geralt est très plaisant à suivre, et c'est un plaisir de voyager dans cet univers avec lui.


[i]Bref, j'ai pris mon temps pour terminer l'intrigue principale, qui si elle n'est pas parfaite en terme de rythme, s'est terminé sur un gros moment d'émotions. Je suis maintenant impatiente d'aller plus haut vers le Nord, découvrir les nouveaux tableaux d'affichages, et entamer Hearts of Stone. J'ai vraiment adoré mon temps dans Witcher 3, et tant d'aventures m'attendent encore ...

Редко какая игра способна настолько органично сочетать глубокий сюжет, проработанный мир, живых персонажей и чувство настоящего приключения. *The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt* — именно такой случай. На момент написания этого отзыва я нахожусь на Скеллиге, и уже сейчас могу с полной уверенностью сказать, что это одна из лучших игр, в которые мне доводилось играть.
Главное достоинство игры — её удивительная способность погружать в собственную вселенную. Каждый квест, даже самый незначительный на первый взгляд, обладает собственной драматургией, моральной неоднозначностью и последствиями. Здесь нет банального разделения на добро и зло — есть лишь сложные решения, каждое из которых оставляет след в судьбах людей и самого мира.
Особенно впечатлил Скеллиге. Суровый архипелаг, омываемый свинцовыми волнами, поражает своей атмосферой. Величественные горные пейзажи, древние традиции островитян, политические интриги и легенды, пронизывающие каждый уголок этих земель, создают ощущение настоящего живого мира. Исследовать эти острова — одно удовольствие.
Отдельной похвалы заслуживают персонажи. Геральт здесь не просто главный герой, а полноценная личность со своими убеждениями и принципами. Второстепенные герои также написаны с редким мастерством: их мотивация понятна, а поступки выглядят естественными и правдоподобными.
Визуальная составляющая даже спустя годы остаётся впечатляющей. Закаты над морем Скеллиге, густые леса Велена и оживлённые улицы Новиграда способны соперничать со многими современными проектами. Музыкальное сопровождение заслуживает отдельных оваций: композиции не просто сопровождают происходящее, а усиливают эмоциональное воздействие каждой сцены.
Конечно, игра не лишена мелких шероховатостей. Иногда встречаются незначительные технические огрехи или спорные элементы боевой системы. Однако на фоне масштаба и качества всего остального эти недостатки выглядят практически незаметными.
The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt — это не просто игра, а выдающееся произведение интерактивного искусства, демонстрирующее, каким должен быть настоящий ролевой проект. Она увлекает, восхищает и заставляет искренне переживать за происходящее.
Оценка: 10/10. Безоговорочный шедевр, который обязан пройти каждый поклонник RPG.

The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt Complete Edition is one of those rare games that completely pulls you into its world. What impressed me most is the depth of the storytelling. Even side quests often feel as meaningful and well-written as the main story, with choices that have real consequences and characters that stay memorable long after you've finished their quests.

The world feels alive and immersive, from the war-torn villages of Velen to the bustling streets of Novigrad and the breathtaking landscapes of Skellige. Every location has its own atmosphere, history, and stories waiting to be discovered.

The Complete Edition adds two incredible expansions that could easily pass as full games on their own. Combined with the outstanding writing, rich lore, and immersive world-building, this is one of the best RPG experiences I've ever played.

Highly recommended for anyone who enjoys deep stories, meaningful choices, and getting lost in a beautifully crafted fantasy world.

The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt – An Immortal Masterpiece of the Gaming Industry

Years after its release, The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt remains more than just a game, but a true cultural phenomenon that deservedly bears the title of masterpiece. Even today, many gaming projects fail to achieve even half of what CD Projekt RED managed to create, immersing us in the dark yet terrifyingly captivating world of Geralt of Rivia.

This isn't just a game; it's a lifetime. The Witcher 3 is that rare work that captivates you with its atmosphere from the very first minute, makes you care about the characters, and keeps you on the edge of your seat until the very end. And that's no exaggeration, as something new awaits around every corner, be it an epic battle, a touching story, or a moral choice with far-reaching consequences.

What makes The Witcher 3 so special?

The plot is a masterfully woven fabric. The story of Geralt, searching for his adopted daughter Ciri, is more than just a series of quests. It's a multifaceted narrative, intertwining political intrigue, personal drama, ancient prophecies, and, of course, the fates of ordinary people. Every character, even the minor ones, is brilliantly written, with their own motivations and inner world. You don't just follow the plot—you become part of it, making decisions that sometimes baffle you and force you to ponder the nature of good and evil.

Monsters are the breath of dark fantasy. The Witcher universe is renowned for its monstrous creatures, and in the third installment, they come to life in all their menacing glory. From nimble werewolves to mighty griffins and vile drowners, each enemy is unique, possessing its own weaknesses and requiring an individual approach. Receiving contracts to kill monsters isn't just a way to earn money; it's a complex investigation, a complex analytical task that requires studying the monster's habits, questioning the locals, and mustering the courage for the decisive battle.

The open world is living and breathing. Velen, Skellige, Novigrad—these locations are striking in their detail and scale. There's no feeling of an empty shell. Every corner of the world is filled with events, chance encounters, secrets, and stories. As you travel through forests, mountains, and bustling cities, you'll constantly stumble upon something interesting. The world of The Witcher 3 doesn't just exist; it lives its own life, responding to the player's actions and filling the gameplay with amazing discoveries.

The combat system is a bloody ballet. Battles in The Witcher 3 are dynamic and spectacular. The use of swords, elixirs, signs, and bombs all create tactical depth. Each encounter requires the player to concentrate, understand their enemies, and skillfully apply their skills.

The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt isn't just a game; it's an experience that will stay with you for a long time. It's a benchmark for how to create role-playing games, paying attention to every detail, from character development to the finest details of the environment. If you haven't played this masterpiece yet, don't miss out. It's a journey you'll never forget.

The Witcher 3 is a game that will stay on the "must play" list for many years to come, for people looking for a story-focused experience.  Side quests make sense in-world, and the emotional weight of NPCs do a lot of heavy lifting in them; many actually will influence and expand on the main story. It's not Fallout 4 when it comes to the interpersonal relationship between Geralt and his adopted daughter, Ciri; there's an emotional connection there that makes you want to see what happens, whether or not you find her. This game emphasizes why story isn't an afterthought in a game, and why game developers should spend time and resources into its crafting. Then, you have the DLCs, which continues the trend, and expands the world in some eerie and dark, or fairytale and whimsical ways, with what is one of the best written villains in all games ever.

«Для меня в игровой индустрии существуют лишь две величины, чей нарративный размах сопоставим с великой литературой. Одна из них — суровая приземленная драма. Но вторая... Вторая — это «Ведьмак 3: Дикая Охота».
Этот проект — не просто игра, это эталонное темное фэнтези, которое берет тебя за душу. В отличие от реалистичных боевиков, «Ведьмак» погружает в многогранный мир славянского фольклора, политических интриг и сложнейших моральных выборов. Эта игра перешагнула статус развлечения, став настоящим произведением искусства. Как и с гениальным книжным романом, финальные титры здесь не ставят точку. Они лишь зарождают желание спустя время вновь открыть эту историю, чтобы заново перечитать судьбы любимых героев».

The Witcher 3 é um daqueles jogos raros que conseguem prender você não apenas pela história principal, mas por tudo o que existe ao redor dela. Entrei esperando um RPG de mundo aberto e encontrei um mundo cheio de personagens memoráveis, decisões difíceis e missões secundárias que muitas vezes são tão boas quanto a campanha principal.

A jornada de Geralt é envolvente do início ao fim, e o jogo faz um excelente trabalho em mostrar que nem toda escolha tem uma resposta certa. Suas decisões têm peso, e isso torna a experiência muito mais imersiva.

O combate pode parecer um pouco datado para quem está acostumado com títulos mais recentes, mas a qualidade da narrativa, da ambientação e da construção do mundo compensa facilmente esse detalhe. É o tipo de jogo que faz você sair do caminho principal apenas para seguir uma missão aleatória e acabar vivendo uma das melhores histórias da sua campanha.

As expansões merecem uma menção especial. Hearts of Stone e Blood and Wine não são apenas conteúdos extras; são experiências que ampliam e enriquecem tudo o que o jogo já faz bem.

Mesmo mais de uma década após o lançamento, The Witcher 3 continua sendo uma referência quando o assunto é RPG de mundo aberto. Se você gosta de boas histórias e personagens bem escritos, dificilmente vai se arrepender.

Vim pela caça aos monstros. Fiquei pelas histórias.
Jogo bom da poha, um dos melhores jogos que joguei na vida, nunca enjoa rejogar, com mods fica melhor ainda.

Ведьмак - это тот случай, когда ты, проходя сюжет не можешь не оглянуться, посмотрев закатное небо; красное солнце Велена, или рассвет в Туссенте. Ведьмак - это когда, спрыгнув с лошади на Скеллиге, ты просто можешь остановиться на горе и слушать прекрасную музыку Севера. Ведьмак - это когда ты вслушиваешься в каждую реплику, в каждый диалог, когда в казалось бы, посредственных диалогах ты можешь услышать очень правильные вещи, ведь игра в первую очередь учит, это идеология жизни. Ведьмак - это когда ты задумываешься о своем выборе, можешь на  протяжении определенного количества времени думать, - "А правильно ли я поступил?". Здесь нет правильного выбора, ты всегда жертвуешь чем либо, или даже кем, репутацией... а кто-то даже душой и сердцем, когда приходится выбирать между меньшим злом и большим, но иногда не получается не выбирать вовсе. 
50 часов основного сюжета завершились ушедшей Цири в Белый Хлад, но плохая ли это концовка? Не знаю, но с уверенностью могу сказать, что самая сильная, ведь эта игра - не про "Вот и сказочке конец". (Еще не успев отойти от гибели Весемира... Опустошение.) Но, ознакомившись с ее получением после прохождения, прохождения без единого спойлера, оказалось, что исход основного сюжета зависит от... рандома. И тут лотерея! Тупое разделение на: ты плохой отец/ты хороший отец. Например, неочевидная сцена в Каэр Морхене, где Цири интересуется Геральтом и как он справляется в трудные минуты, но по мнению поляков, "правильный выбор: - "Я знаю что тебя развеселит", оказывается, это является правильнее, чем "Расслабься. Не обязательно быть лучшей во всём", (оказывается это «плохая» фраза). Хотя первая реплика вообще по смыслу является не очень контекстной, плюс в каком ключе ее скажет Геральт и что он имеет ввиду? Чего только стоит "Сильно оттолкнуть Дийкстру". Перемещаемся в лабораторию Авалакх'а, где ты выбираешь успокоить Цири, после чего она говорит... "Ты даже не хочешь меня успокаивать". (опять я плохой). Разве по мнению CDPR: "А ты попробуй", звучит не агрессивнее? Да возьми ты и разгроми сама. Да и сцена с чародейками, казалось бы, посредственный диалог, но тоже влияющий на концовку. Хотя, пойдя вместе с Цири ты "поступил плохо" но при этом если с ней не идти, то мы начинаем за ней с Йеннифер подглядывать, но это - "поступил хорошо". И в двух исходах ситуаций Цири остается в подавленном и грустном состоянии, как тогда это должно влиять? 
Жалко, что исходом вышеперечисленного являются не выборы, а их последствия, почти не имеющие связи с самим выбором. Хотя, возможно, проблемы с локализацией диалогов этому поспособствовали, с чем мы уже сталкивались... Не знаю. И, в конечном итоге, посетив могилу очень важного для Цири человека, Скьялля, и не взяв деньги у Эмгыра, что является самым важным по отношению к ней, а из-за тупой системы выборов в диалогах  я получаю плохую концовку. 
Конечно, поляки облажались, но эта игра не была бы такой великой, если бы не DLC. Чего только стоят "Каменные сердца", чего только стоит разговор с Ирис, мрачная концовка с загадкой О'Дима, да чего только стоит он сам! Самый запоминающийся персонаж. поистине G.O.D. 
А "Кровь и вино", - вообще отдельная игра. Это именно тот случай, когда DLC в 2-3 десятка часов лучше основной сюжетной линии. Чего только стоит сюжет! (Исход которого правда зависит от покупки предмета за 500 крон... кхм). Да и чего только стоит сам Туссент, что является некой медитацией после мрачных болот Велена, чего только стоит озвучка Детлаффа, разговор с Регисом в концовке... До мурашек! 
Тот случай, когда зайдешь поиграть на полчаса, а выйдешь через три. 
Хочется стереть память, пройти заново. Но лучше пройти первый раз и больше не получать настолько сильных эмоций, количества мурашек в кат-сценах с Дикой Охотой, чем не проходить вовсе. Такова реальность. 
Спасибо, Анджей Сапковский. Спасибо, CD Project. Отдельное спасибо, команда русской озвучки, нет ни одной другой локализации с такой харизмой, как у вас. Спасибо музыкантам. Спасибо фанатам. Спасибо всем причастным к этом шедевру!

7/10. Not a game that I would recommend in 2026 but nonetheless I enjoyed it. Playing this game in 2026 feels like a game that you are playing a less polished game which a bunch of QoL issues. However given this game is made in 2015, that is understandable. I would give credit to the game on its character writing (strongest point), music and voice acting. Everything else in the game feels outdated. I do like the idea of having 3 main endings however I would appreciate it more if the developer does give some hints as to which of the dialogue options that would ultimately affect the endings as I felt there are so many dialogue options given but only like 5 of them actually matters to the final ending.

The character progression system in the game is a bad game design in my opinion. This is because you are capped by 12 skill slot when there is 80 skills to choose from. Further more, these 80 skills is split into 4 section and within each section is a skill tree. This means you need to invest certain skill points into the skill tree before unlocking better skills. However there is no telling of how good the skills is as there is no skill preview of how the skill works and how effective it is. There is also no reset skill tree option if you want to try other type of skills. Basically you are stucked with the same skills throughout the game. I did not touch on the gear crafting section of the game as it once again is another mess. Some gear requires certain higher level NPC to craft them and the game does not tell you where to locate this NPC. There is also no tracking in the game regarding the crafting item required which makes crafting equipment a headache.

I am also not the biggest fan of the side quest in the game which revolves around the events of the main quest. The side quest is missable if you advance to far into the main story and certain side quest will be affected by the choices you made during the main quest. However, I do give credits to the side quest as it does keep me intrigued and it is not boring and repetitive like the side games in the other games.

The combat of the game felt okay and nothing too innovative in my opinion. You basically just smash the same two buttons of dodge, hit, dodge, hit when it comes to facing the bosses as the magic spells takes too long to cast.

Fair to say that the combat is awful. It was corrupting my enjoyment so much that i had to play on story mode difficulty. Enough said, given that i usually play on the maximum difficulty. A shame, since by its looks it appeared to be great. Though it does grow much less brutal as you level up.

Horse riding is very clunky; 80% of the time i prefered to run to my destination rather than ride. 

Impressive how the game is so jam-packed with content but it doesn't ever get quite banal and every encounter seems more or less unique, unlike how it is in other RPGs (Skyrim, AC Origins, Odyssey and Valhalla, Starfield ect.).

All in all completely outstanding worldbuilding, story, OST, graphics and character nuance.

The Witcher 3 é uma experiência que vai muito além de um simples videogame. A história é fantástica, os personagens são memoráveis, as escolhas realmente importam e até as missões secundárias têm uma qualidade impressionante. Poucos jogos conseguem criar um mundo tão vivo e imersivo.

E quando você acha que o jogo já entregou tudo, chegam as DLCs. Hearts of Stone e Blood and Wine são absurdamente boas, cada uma conseguindo elevar ainda mais o nível de um jogo que já parecia uma obra-prima.

O que mais me marcou foi a sensação ao terminar tudo: fiquei genuinamente triste por ter acabado. Depois de tantas horas naquele mundo, parecia uma despedida. Pouquíssimos jogos conseguem causar esse sentimento.

The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt remains one of the best open-world RPGs. The true magic of the game lies in its incredible sense of immersion and deep, authentic lore. Every village feels lived-in, and even the smallest side quests are written with the care of a main storyline.

If you are an experienced gamer, I highly recommended playing on the hardest difficulty, especially if you plan on using a guide or looking up builds. Because coming prepared with the right oils, potions, and signs completely changes the dynamic of a fight, preparation can make the game feel significantly easier. Upping the difficulty preserves the tension and rewards that preparation. I enjoyed reading and learning about the monsters while preparing. It built up tension before a big fight.

However, if you are a completionist aiming for that 100% run, be prepared for a bit of a slog. Tracking down every single Gwent card across the continent can turn from a fun tactical minigame into a genuinely tedious chore. I would like to note that I have the platinum trophy on playstation and will eventually get it on PC as well - so it's not THAT bad!

If you play it to get lost in an unforgettable world and enjoy a strategic combat challenge, it’s easily a 10/10.

Провів у грі вже 17 годин і можу сказати, що The Witcher 3 повністю виправдала свою репутацію. Найбільше сподобалися сюжет, цікаві персонажі та атмосфера світу. Кожне завдання відчувається важливим, а побічні квести часто не менш цікаві, ніж основна історія. Бойова система хоч і не ідеальна, але достатньо динамічна, щоб не набридати.

Сильні сторони:
✔ Захопливий сюжет і добре прописані персонажі.
✔ Величезний відкритий світ із безліччю активностей.
✔ Чудова музика та атмосфера.
✔ Побічні завдання зроблені на дуже високому рівні.
✔ Гарна графіка навіть через багато років після релізу.

Слабкі сторони:
✘ Управління іноді здається незручним.
✘ Бойова система може здатися одноманітною після багатьох годин гри.
✘ Деякі механіки, наприклад пересування на коні, працюють не завжди ідеально.

Загалом гра мені дуже сподобалась. За 17 годин я ще не пройшов її повністю, але вже розумію, чому багато хто вважає її однією з найкращих RPG. Однозначно рекомендую.
10/10. One of my favourite games of all time. The story, characters, visuals, atmosphere. It's perfect.
神，伟大无需多言。11年前的顶级开放世界游戏，11年后还要更新大型资料片回馈玩家。推荐去看看原著小说猎魔人再玩游戏。另外说下，刚开始玩不下去觉得无聊很正常，我看了原著猎魔人三遍，最开始玩了五次，下下删删都玩不下去。后来慢慢体验，尝试沉浸才发觉这游戏真是顶级艺术品。期待巫师4.

我主玩动作游戏，也是个剧情党，第一次接触巫师系列，本作给我的感觉非常割裂。
最烂的有2点，首先就是战斗系统，入坑前就听说很烂，结果确实如此，烂的我直接开宗门一刀过，虽然有很多流派build但是底层战斗框架拖后腿太严重，就算构筑好了进战斗以后也就那几下，打击感没有，武器就一两个动作模组，拿另一个偏科生arpg尼尔机械纪元对比，虽然战斗也很无聊也是一样靠数值碾压，但本身动作很流畅，打击感和动作设计都很在线。
第二就是骑马手感稀烂，byd头一次见马还能倒着骑的，怕不是直接拿摩托的模型改的，别的游戏往后推摇杆马会直接掉头，在这只会慢吞吞往后退，而且稍微碰到一点障碍就过不去强制停下，必须手动跳过去，自动寻路碰到岔路的时候经常跑歪。
至于开放世界设计和育碧的罐头如出一辙，无非就是把支线任务隐藏了必须靠近才能触发，但还是充斥大量的通马桶和问号，我还全程没做收集省下不少时间。任务设计也很落后，80%的任务都是对话-跑路-跟踪脚印-砍怪-对话，全程除了对话不需要动一点脑子，而且几乎全是站桩对话，没有演出。
叙事和世界观塑造没得说，任务结局分支多的吓人，但又回到先前说的，想体验本作的故事必须花费一大半的时间在无聊透顶的战斗和跑路上，所以本质上就是个大型3d开放世界galgame，全靠文本量和细节力大砖飞，就算把战斗和探索去掉也不影响核心体验。
游戏一开始确实有点难上手，我没玩过前两代，而且还有点阴森恐怖，玩了几个小时就放下了。后来有空了又重新玩，才发现这个游戏的精彩，剧情相当的好，而且人物角色都很漂亮，魔法的世界很吸引人，一口气通关。

如何在快节奏叙事下让玩家迅速对眼前的地区/章节限定角色产生感情永远是大部分国产手游无法跨越的一座大山，我觉得cdpr每次对这种事情的处理都做得很好，无论在2077还是在巫师3。哪怕完全不了解巫师1、2的剧情，也能自然而然的对性格鲜明的角色迅速产生兴趣，而不是当成千篇一律的过场工具人，完全不会让人感到乏味麻木。剧情应该是围绕角色展开，为角色服务的，而不是让角色服务剧情。单纯为了冲突设计一个角色完全是本末倒置。可能不是我玩过游戏里剧情最打动我的，但在我心里绝对有不轻的重量。W cdpr，赶紧把旧时曲端上来吧
说实话，这游戏挺没意思的，我只敢在舍友在的时候玩，毕竟走着走着出来一个女夜魔挺害怕的

在遊玩超過 100 小時後，我仍然覺得《巫師 3：狂獵》是一款非常值得推薦的作品。
最讓我印象深刻的是遊戲對劇情選擇的自由度。許多看似不起眼的對話選項，都有可能影響後續任務的發展甚至角色的命運，讓每一次選擇都充滿思考與代入感，而不是單純按照固定劇本推進。
除了劇情之外，遊戲世界也相當龐大且充滿細節。無論是在主線任務、支線故事，還是探索地圖的過程中，都能發現許多精彩的小故事與人物遭遇。每個地區都有自己的文化、背景與問題，讓整個世界顯得非常真實且有生命力。
即使是多年前推出的作品，放到現在來看仍然是一款劇情、世界觀與遊戲性兼具的經典 RPG。非常推薦喜歡劇情導向、角色扮演與開放世界探索的玩家體驗。

It's definitely story driven. It's not your typical hack-slash quest line game. Every quest is connected to a larger story line. After you battle, you are sometimes given an animated update of the main story. I do like that when you return, the game gives you a 'so far this is what has happened', which you could skip, if you don't need the information, but, it's nice for when you take a long break, you can say, oh, yeah, that's what I was doing the last time.... There are side quests and secondary quests and witcher contracts. There is mini-game, a card game, Gwent, which I am *not* at all interested in, but some people might be.  If you enjoy fantasy, reading stories, and maybe a little bit of anime, you will likely like this game.

Ведьмак 3 — это игра которая однажды просто въезжает в твою жизнь, ставит вещи в угол и остаётся насовсем. Мир живёт без тебя — крестьяне ругаются на погоду, торговцы жульничают, где-то в таверне пьяный бард орёт балладу про Геральта которому это явно неприятно. Побочные квесты здесь лучше чем главный сюжет в большинстве других игр — ты заходишь «помочь мужику найти козу» и через час стоишь над моральным выбором от которого реально плохо на душе. Цири пропала, Дикая Охота наступает, мир рушится — а ты сидишь в таверне и режешься в гвинт с каким-то мужиком уже третий час потому что он не отдаёт нужную карту. Геральт ворчит, Йеннифэр манипулирует, Цири врывается в сердце и не спрашивает разрешения — персонажи живые настолько что расставаться с ними физически больно. А Кровавый Барон — это отдельная история про сломанного человека, трагедию и боль, финал которой буквально вишенка на торте, а может быть на дереве. Грустно смеёшься и ненавидишь себя за это. Игра не заканчивается когда ты проходишь финал — она просто остаётся где-то внутри и иногда напоминает о себе.
Birçok oyun oynanama rağmen bu oyun benim için çok ayrı bir yerde bu tarz sevenlere kesinlikle öneriyorum süper bir yaratıclık ve devamını sabırsızlıkla bekliyorum...

Another Goat of mine, another game I played the crap out of on console and then had to cop it on pc... This is in my mount Rushmore of gaming, one of my favorites of all time, I've beaten and redid the main story at least 4 times, and have done almost every side quest that I came across, so across my 380 hours of gameplay, here's my review for The Witcher 3

Unlike the previous Witcher games You don't have to be familiar with the series to get into it, it does an amazing job at introducing those that may be unfamiliar with the world into its atmosphere without completely overwhelming them, it did a great job at separating itself as a 'good game' and not just a 'good Witcher's game'. And the characters are amazing, it's to the point where even the people you don't like, antagonists, small times villains in a side quest, all have a weird depth to them if you adventure and look for it

The story: of course being apart of The Witcher's series is amazing, one of the best gaming stories and endings you can find, without getting too into detail just know it's amazing

Gameplay: It's pretty similar to the elder scrolls, particularly Oblivion as far as ability usage and magic usage goes, and without spoiling, I can just say that's the baseline, expect the combat to be a bit of Elder Scrolls Oblivion and Souls-like, and as far as difficulty goes, it's pretty challenging

Exploration: It's one of those games where you get so immersed in just adventuring that you completely forget the whole reason you went to an area was to complete a main quest, you can get pretty good and high quality gear from dungeons and exploring, so  exploration is pretty rewarding.

That's just about everything that stands out, My rating:
Story: 10/10
Exploration: 10/10
Combat:8.5/10
Graphics: 7.5/10 (can be pretty but the game came out a little while ago so it's not the best looking, but recently they've added some mods and a new gen update to it, so it may have gotten better since I last played)
Overall: 9/10
This is the best review I could do without spoiling too much and ruining the experience, I truly believe this is one of those games you go into blind and find the beauty in it

If you like RPGs TW3 is a must play. The setting and the scenery that you can find within it are absolutely stunning, despite it being more than 10 yrs old. The story is nothing special to be honest, but the characters that you will meet and the quests related to them will make it worth it. This is true especially in the DLCs where the game improves in every aspect: quests, characters, combat, story, direction. Also the setting is really enhanced with the inspiration that it has taken from the polish folklore, especially the music. I suggest you checking out the band Percival that gave their input and composed some songs for the game and you can really feel its impact on the vibes.
When talking about the gameplay I have to say that the combat is lacking, I feel like that it was built around the idea of making it as realistic as possible, but when you're actually playing it you can feel the stiffness that come with realism. Simple movements like turning around, or getting on a boat becomes a gargantuan challenge, I would have preferred a more "arcade" combat, leaning towards a souls-like.
The world is open and really vast but it is not as dense in content, sure there are some "spots" where you can find treasures but their content is never rewarding, considering the time spent to reach them. Basically looking for them feels more like a waste of time, unfortunately.
The last downside that I have to talk about is the lack of context, if you have not read the books you will still understand the story but you will miss out so much. I decided to read the first book before playing the Blood and Wine and I have to say that the experience was enhanced so much. Maybe in this DLC they decided to quote more the books but I could really feel the difference.
Concluding the game, despite its weakness, is an absolutely gem, especially if we consider the time of its original release.

I remember how excited I was leading up to the release of The Witcher 3. I watched the developer diaries style videos that CD Projekt released periodically in the months before the game released and I couldn't believe it was real, it seemed too good to be true at the time. Then May 2015 came and it completely blew me away.

Does it have flaws? Absolutely, as any game does. The combat feels weightless and silly at times, the character movement for Geralt and Roach are both as clunky as anything else I've played. The inventory system is famously bad.

But in between all those flaws are some of the best characters, quests and moments that I have ever experienced in a video game.

To put it plainly, It is comfortably one of the best games I have ever played.

The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt is a golden standard for RPG games and open world games. It has been said every time but the side missions are as meaningful, impactful as the main quests. The characters are well written and their is a history between everyone that you do have to catch up on but it feels like you are visiting a moment in these peoples lives. It's wonderful. 

It is however a 11 year old game at this point and truthfully it is impressive as ever and still pretty impressive today. The gameplay holds up mostly. I love that the combat does not retort to spongy enemies but it does feel a little overly simple and can definitely be improved for the upcoming 4th game. It also tends to drag a little as it goes on, the story stays compelling but the gameplay does little to really stay interesting. If the combat system gets fleshed out in the next one, Ill be gone in the world for a very long time. The skill tree also needs work. It really does not feel that meaningful when you are putting stuff in the 'tree'.

Однозначно рекомендую. Шедевр на все времена.
Как и в случае со многими культовыми играми, мое знакомство с третьим «Ведьмаком» началось на релизе с пиратки. Интернета тогда по физическим причинам не было, прикоснуться к главной игре года хотелось безумно. Проходил в полном одиночестве, без гайдов и патчей, но атмосфера затянула с головой. Сейчас, имея стабильный интернет и лицензию со всеми дополнениями, проходить эту историю - отдельный вид кайфа.
Это эталон того, какими должны быть RPG. Прошло больше десяти лет с релиза, а лучше так никто и не сделал.
Итоговые оценки:
Сюжет и квесты: 11 из 10. Главный квест - это, конечно, хорошо, но все мы знаем, что спасение Цири подождет, пока мы не соберем абсолютно все карты для Гвинта у каждого встречного корчмаря.
Геймплей: 9 из 10. Отличный баланс между боевкой и алхимией. И отдельный «поклон» разработчикам за прекрасные знаки вопросов на карте. Особенно «круто» и «весело» собирать их на Скеллиге. Перегруз, тонны контрабанды, бесконечные лодки и сирены, которые их ломают, - истинное ведьмачье удовольствие, от которого дергается глаз. И не только он, если вы понимаете, о чем я.
Графика и атмосфера: 10 из 10. Закаты в Велене, величие Новиграда и суровые пейзажи Скеллиге до сих пор заставляют останавливаться и делать скриншоты. Музыкальное сопровождение - чистый восторг.
Дополнения: 12 из 10. «Каменные сердца» и «Кровь и вино» по объему и качеству истории превосходят многие отдельные современные игры. Однозначно стоят каждой копейки.

I will preface this review by saying that whatever my playtime shows, you can add about 400 hours between switch and playstation, I have indeed played this game super religiously. Now, this game to me is like what all my friends that I had in elementary school thought of skyrim. It is an amazing fantasy game with plenty of room for buildcrafting and replayability and is an excellent entry point into the series. Most people start with this one as the previous two are very dated. You can lean heavy into potions and do a build that strengthens you as you build toxicity (a mechanic used to keep you from downing all 30 or so different potions before a battle and just steamrolling everything on even the hardest difficulties) or you can make a build that makes you spin in circles as you cut down your enemies. It has a beautiful open world with plenty of villages, settlements, and other large places to visit and a great wilderness filled with monsters and peaceful creatures alike. And if all that isn't enough, there's gwent. If you don't know gwent, then you've likely been under a rock as most people and their grandmas know gwent. An addicting minigame in which you collect cards by finding them in the world, buying them off of merchants, or winning them by defeating opponents in gwent. A simple one, but still absolutely addicting. And the story is amazing. You may even find yourself returning for another playthrough or thirty. There's a choice system that causes the ability for you to experience different outcomes to many quests. Early on there is a quest where you can choose between a couple outcomes and based on how you choose, someone may die or be seriously negatively impacted, but the alternative could be watching an entire village being wiped off the map. Or later on you might choose to honor a prior agreement or disregard it at the request of a friend, and that could lead to hurting that friend's feelings or even an alternate ending to the whole main story. Your choices have an actual impact on the course of your story with some side quests even deciding the state of your world as the main story progresses. You can easily sink a couple hundred hours into just a single playthrough, and that's not even addressing the two excellent story expansions that each add a large amount to the game. Not to mention that you could do three or four runs of the game and its expansions before running out of unique outcomes to both individual quests and the overall plot. And with a third story expansion, songs of the past, on the horizon, there is truly no better time to get immersed in the amazing world of the witcher.

Hmm… Nereden başlasam bilemedim.

Öncelikle çeşit çeşit yaratıklarla kılıç ve büyü kullanarak savaştığınız o karanlık Witcher evrenine hoş geldiniz. Oynanış tarafında, günümüzde hâlâ sırıtmayacak kadar akıcı bir savaş sistemi var. Özellikle yakın dövüş mekanikleri bana yer yer Batman Arkham oyunlarını hatırlatıyor. Sürekli hareket ederek, sağa sola kaçıp düşmanları biçtiğiniz akıcı bir combat yapısına sahip.

Ama Witcher 3’ü özel yapan şey sadece savaş sistemi değil. Haritayı keşfetmek, yan görevler ve ana hikâye derken oyun sizi resmen içine çekiyor. Hatta çoğu yan görev, başka oyunlarda ana görev olacak kalitede yazılmış.

Bir de üstüne küçük oyun demeye utandıracak kadar dolu 2 büyük DLC’si var. Özellikle hikâye tarafında oyunun seviyesini daha da yukarı taşıyorlar. Benim için yeri gerçekten ayrı oyunlardan biridir.

Açık dünyası büyük ama boş değil. Haritada gördüğünüz soru işaretlerine gidince bazen küçük hikâyeler, bazen yaratık avları, bazen de beklenmedik olaylarla karşılaşıyorsunuz. Sürekli “şurada ne var acaba?” hissi yaşatıyor.

Eğer karakter geliştirmeli, görev çeşitliliği bol ve atmosferi güçlü oyunlar hoşunuza gidiyorsa, Witcher 3’e kesinlikle bir şans vermenizi tavsiye ederim.

The Witcher 3 não é só um jogo, é uma experiência absurda do começo ao fim. Pouquíssimos games conseguem criar um mundo tão vivo, detalhado e imersivo quanto esse. Cada cidade, floresta, vila ou pântano parece ter história própria, e até as missões secundárias têm mais profundidade e qualidade narrativa do que campanhas principais de muitos jogos atuais.

Geralt é facilmente um dos protagonistas mais bem escritos da indústria, e o jeito como o jogo desenvolve os personagens faz você realmente se importar com cada escolha. E falando nisso: as escolhas aqui importam de verdade. Não existe “certo” ou “errado”, apenas consequências — muitas vezes pesadas e emocionantes.

A trilha sonora é simplesmente perfeita. Basta ouvir alguns segundos para entrar completamente no clima do continente. O combate, os contratos de bruxo, os monstros, os diálogos, a ambientação… tudo parece feito com um nível absurdo de carinho e dedicação.

E as DLCs? Blood and Wine praticamente é um jogo novo de tão gigantesca e impecável que é. Hearts of Stone entrega uma das melhores histórias já feitas em um RPG.

The Witcher 3 é aquele tipo de jogo que deixa vazio quando termina, porque você percebe que viveu algo especial. Uma verdadeira obra-prima da CD Projekt Red e, sem exagero, um dos melhores jogos já criados.

It happened around 10 to 20 times throughout my playthrough, which is annoying, though I don't expect patches at this point so it's something I can live with.
I highly recommend picking up the DLCs, as they add so much to the overall experience.
Are the graphics slowly becoming outdated? Maybe. But it's one of the most atmospheric, well written and enjoyable RPG out there.

Well well well... This is the first game i have ever bought in steam and i am glad to say i am very much satisfied. 

[h3]Story:[/h3]
I have not yet completed the story yet but so far i would say its not boring at least. Some of the dialogues and interactions were kinda bland but the overall story is pretty good with lot of choices to make and its interesting to see that our decisions kinda affect the storyline and the world. 

[h3]The MC[/h3]

Geralt is the best MC i have come across in video games. The dialog choices, the voice acting, and his mannerisms all are badass. One of the main reason i dont find the story boring is geralt himself. His interactions were always fun.

[h3]Atmosphere and world[/h3]
 
The last RPG i played was skyrim and that game had a very impressive open world. Now after playing TW3, Skyrim map seem like a child's play now. After exploring Novigrad and oxenfurt in TW3 , Whiterun and falkreath seem like small villages now. The NPC interactions , the way things are staged in the map everything is very impressive. ofc if you interact with random NPC's, they do repeat the same dialogue but its forgivable.

The weather system is dynamic, immersive, and ofc those sunsets and sunrises ! They  were some of the most impressive i have ever seen. Just riding through Skellige islands with the music on was so soothing to the eyes and ears.

[h3]Graphics and performance[/h3]

I played this game on a integrated Gpu , the radeon 680m (single channel) with the following settings:

Resolution:720p upscaled to 1200p RSR
Graphics setting: Everything on medium except water and Bg characters(both were set to high)
AA: TAA
DirectX: 11

With these graphics settings the game looked pretty impressive to my eyes and ran at average of 37 FPS. Of course with at higher Graphics it would look even damn better.

[h3]Overall[/h3]

if you liked skyrim or similar RPG's and haven't played witcher 3 you must give it a go! I was able to buy it for Rs.500 ( $5) and it is one of the best purchases i have made .  this game indeed is one of the greatest open world games

only 8 hours in and this game has wowed me. first of all, it looks amazing. the graphics are a bit out of date but it doesnt matter because everything contributes to the world's feeling. on the same note, the narrative is rich and pretty easy to understand - im saying this having never played any other witcher games. 

my one negative is the combat system and learning curve. geralt LOVESSS to not listen to your controls and just flail around. the timing on the strong and weak attacks changes like half the time. sometimes, geralt takes a step forward before he strikes and sometimes hes too lazy to do that. other than that, this game rocks.

This game is an absolute masterpiece and the blueprint for what an open-world RPG should be. The storytelling is flawless, making even the smallest side quests feel impactful, morally grey, and deeply personal. It genuinely respects your intelligence, forcing you to live with the heavy consequences of your choices without any hand-holding. Visually, the world is stunning, and the Slavic soundtrack is phenomenal.
The combat can feel a bit repetitive once you figure out a broken build, but the preparation aspect—oils, potions, and signs—keeps the Witcher fantasy alive. Best of all, it is incredibly well-optimized. If you are running a total potato or an ancient low-end PC, just switch the launch options to DirectX 11 or roll back to the "Classic" version in the Steam Beta tab, and it will run smooth as butter while still looking amazing.
Honestly, you might just end up ignoring the main quest for hours just to play Gwent with random tavern owners. If you haven't played this yet, stop reading and buy it. It is worth every single penny.

Termine este juego como en el 2016 o 2017 y nunca escribí una reseña.

Un poco tarde pero aquí está (mientras esperamos por el nuevo DLC y The Witcher 4).

Comenzaré diciendo que The Witcher 3 esta en el top 5 de los mejores juegos que he jugado en mi vida. Aun no decido en que posición está, pero es uno de esos 5 seguro.

Este juego tiene muchas cualidades, para empezar la historia. De verdad que la historia y los personajes de este juego están muy bien escritos. Todo el tiempo me mantenía interesado y a la expectativa de que iba a pasar después, era como ver una serie muy buena. Además hay algunos arcos que no voy a spoilear (como el del Barón) que son buenísimos, con sus giros argumentales y algunos incluso emotivos y emocionantes.

Realmente le agarra uno cariño a los personajes, en especial a Geralt y Ciri que son mis favoritos, aunque hay muchísimos mas personajes excelentes en la historia de este juego.

Tiene muchísimas misiones secundarias, pero con la gran diferencia de que aquí (a diferencia de la mayoría de mundos abiertos donde las misiones secundarias son genéricas y repetitivas) en este juego cada misión secundaria tiene su propia historia y personajes, con sus propios giros y sorpresas. Nada de misiones genéricas repetitivas (y lo se porque las hice TODAS).

Gráficamente, el juego se ve impresionante para ser un juego que salió en el 2015. Incluso para los estándares de hoy en día se sigue viendo genial.

La jugabilidad, excelente. Hay gente que critica un poco el sistema de combate pero yo la verdad nunca tuve problema con el. Me parece que funciona perfecto para este juego.

Los dos DLCs igual de geniales que el juego base, en especial la de Blood and Wine que incluso añade algunas novedades al juego, como tener tu propia villa y poder mejorarla.

Si tuviera que darle algo negativo al juego seria tal vez que el ritmo de la historia en ocasiones se vuelve lento. No es siempre, solo en arcos concretos. Pero es algo que ocurre solo de forma esporádica. La historia en general es tan buena que esa pequeña inconveniencia se vuelve insignificante.

En resumen, este juego es uno de los mejores RPG que he jugado, con algunos de los mejores personajes y una de las mejores historias. No por nada este fue el juego que catapulto a CDPR. (Junto con The Witcher 2)

Si no lo has jugado, hazlo, así sea solo por la historia. Uno de los mejores juegos que he jugado, fácil.

I finally played what many people call the GOAT of RPG's and I have to agree with them. From the story to the graphics, music, gameplay, and consequential decision making. All of it comes together to deliver this great game and for the age it is, it delivers. Highly recommend it to anyone looking for a story driven action packed game!

The Witcher 3 es fácilmente uno de los mejores RPG que jugué. Lo que más sorprende no es solamente el tamaño del juego, sino la calidad de absolutamente todo: personajes, diálogos, ambientación, música y misiones.

Hay quests secundarias que tienen más alma y mejor escritura que campañas principales de otros juegos. El mundo se siente vivo, oscuro y real, y las decisiones realmente generan impacto. Geralt termina siendo un protagonista increíble, rodeado de personajes memorables.

Cada región tiene su propia identidad y atmósfera, desde los pantanos de Velen hasta las islas de Skellige. Explorar nunca se vuelve pesado porque siempre aparece alguna historia interesante, un contrato, un misterio o algo que termina desviándote completamente del objetivo principal.

El combate quizá no sea perfecto al inicio, pero cuando empezás a combinar señales, pociones, aceites y habilidades, se vuelve muchísimo más entretenido.

Incluso después de tantos años, sigue viéndose hermoso y manteniendo un nivel de detalle impresionante.

Una experiencia obligatoria para cualquiera que ame los RPG.

No game is perfect, but I've played over 1,000 hours of this game.  Do you think I think it's any good?  It's one of my top five (5) favorite titles of all time.  A deep, rich, open-world Medieval fantasy set in the universe of Polish author, Andrzej Sapkowski.  Blood and Wine is literally the best DLC ever made for ANY video game, not just the Witcher series.  

If you want to be inspired by your own Art of any kind; writing, graphics, sound design, horror, and beyond, then have fun and enjoy this title, because it inspires and has been inspired by the best.  This is CD Projekt Red at their peak.  Before they sold out as a Publicly Traded Company.

Jogar The Witcher 3 anos após o lançamento só prova o quanto este jogo é atemporal. Mesmo com o peso da idade em alguns pequenos aspectos técnicos, a jornada de Geralt de Rívia continua engolindo a maioria dos RPGs modernos com extrema facilidade.

O que torna o jogo inesquecível:
Narrativa Impecável: Até as missões secundárias mais simples têm roteiros absurdamente bem escritos, com escolhas morais cinzentas que realmente fazem você parar e pensar.

Construção de Mundo: Um universo vivo, denso e melancólico. Explorar o Continente — de Velen a Skellige — é uma experiência incrivelmente imersiva.

Personagens Marcantes: A dinâmica entre o Geralt, a Ciri e a Yennefer (ou Triss) carrega o jogo com um peso emocional raríssimo de encontrar na indústria.

Se você, assim como eu, demorou anos para dar uma chance a este clássico, não espere mais. É um daqueles raros jogos que deixam um "vazio" enorme quando terminam. Uma obra-prima obrigatória para qualquer fã de videogames.

Nota: 10/10 (E não deixe de jogar as DLCs, Hearts of Stone e Blood and Wine, que conseguem ser tão boas ou até melhores que a campanha principal).

Open world RPG costruito attorno a una cosa molto semplice: raccontare storie fatte bene.

Il loop di gioco è quello classico — esplorazione, combattimenti, investigazione — ma il vero valore non sta nel sistema in sé, quanto in quello che succede mentre lo usi. Ogni quest, anche secondaria, è scritta come se fosse importante. E spesso lo è.

Il combat system è funzionale, a tratti ripetitivo, e non è mai stato il punto forte del gioco. Ma regge il tutto senza mai diventare un vero problema, perché viene costantemente spinto in secondo piano dalla qualità della narrazione e del mondo.

L’open world è enorme, ma soprattutto “vivo” nella percezione: villaggi, strade, mostri e NPC non sono solo contenuto, ma pezzi di un contesto coerente. Alcune zone sono più riuscite di altre, ma l’insieme mantiene sempre una forte identità.

Il vero salto di qualità è nella scrittura: missioni principali e secondarie hanno spesso più peso emotivo di interi giochi. I DLC alzano ulteriormente l’asticella, con contenuti che tranquillamente potrebbero essere giochi a sé.

Non è un gioco perfetto. Il combat resta semplice, alcune meccaniche RPG sono grezze e l’open world a tratti si dilata troppo. Ma quando funziona, funziona a un livello che pochi altri titoli raggiungono.

È uno di quei giochi che non si valutano solo per “come sono fatti”, ma per cosa ti lasciano addosso quando smetti di giocarci.

The Witcher 3 is a massive, story-driven RPG that’s easy to get lost in - though not without some fatigue along the way. I originally came into it as my first Witcher game, and with that I had no trouble following the story, as it does a great job getting you up to speed. The narrative is one of its biggest strengths, with strong characters and a more focused, personal approach compared to something like Skyrim. While I still prefer the freedom and character customization of Skyrim, this is a very worthy entry in the action fantasy genre.

From a technical standpoint, it’s impressive. The world looks great, runs smoothly, and generally feels polished. Combat is solid, and monster hunting is easily the highlight of the entire experience - it’s where the game feels most focused and rewarding. Riding horseback, on the other hand, felt a bit clunky, and I often preferred traveling on foot just to avoid the hassle.

Where the game starts to stumble is in its sheer size. There’s so much content here that it can feel overwhelming. Side quests are plentiful - sometimes to a fault - and while many are well-crafted, others begin to feel repetitive or like filler. I’d complete several quests only to unlock even more, which eventually led to burnout. The main story also starts strong but loses some momentum in its later stages, which made it easier to drift away into side content instead of pushing forward. Another minor gripe is the lack of environmental variety. While the world is beautifully designed, much of it shares a similar look and tone, lacking the kind of biome diversity that makes exploration feel more dynamic.

Still, despite these issues, The Witcher 3 is undeniably a great game. It’s ambitious, content-rich, and full of memorable moments. It just might be too much of a good thing at times.

Nowadays, I often just fire it up to play Gwent for an hour or two. I think it's definitely my favorite in-game game ever developed. I even own a set IRL.
